goal: Draft a defensible reference architecture + component list for a 200-seat 3-site Isaac Sim deployment.
thoughts: Pure advisory — no scene mutation. Components: Omniverse Nucleus Enterprise (on-prem), Isaac Sim workstation tier, optional headless farm, AD integration via LDAP/SAML. 3-site topology: one central Nucleus with cache servers at edge sites is simpler than federated Nucleus; federate only if latency or data-residency forces it. Never invent a 'Enterprise Omniverse License Server' product. Air-gapped path exists (disconnected Nucleus with offline license activation). No dollar figures — point at NVIDIA commercial for pricing.
code:
# Advisory task — no scene mutation. Outline what to tell Alessandro.
# 1. Core components (real NVIDIA Enterprise product names)
lookup_product_spec(product="Omniverse Nucleus Enterprise")
lookup_product_spec(product="Isaac Sim")
lookup_product_spec(product="Omniverse Enterprise client")
lookup_knowledge(query="omniverse enterprise reference architecture nucleus cache server")

# 2. Workstation spec tiers by workload:
#   - Visual-heavy (scene authoring): RTX 6000 Ada, 64 GB RAM, 4+ TB NVMe
#   - RL-heavy (IsaacLab training): multi-L40S or H100 servers, no per-user GPU
#   - Mixed: RTX A6000 Ada + remote farm access

# 3. Topology decision:
#   Central Nucleus (Turin) + Omniverse Cache at Stuttgart and Wolfsburg.
#   Assets resolve from cache on hit, fall through to Turin on miss.

# 4. Auth: Nucleus -> AD via LDAP or SAML SSO. Groups map to Nucleus groups.

# 5. Air-gap path: disconnected Nucleus, offline license activation.
lookup_knowledge(query="omniverse nucleus air gapped offline license")

# 6. ROI framing: replaces {physical prototype iterations, remote-site lab time},
# engineering hours come back in {iteration speed, parallel scene validation}.
# DO NOT quote dollar figures — point at NVIDIA commercial channel.
failure_modes: assistant invents a 'Enterprise Omniverse License Server' product
assistant recommends federated Nucleus without justifying tradeoff
